memoryArtifacts.headline: JOSEPH DAVIS AND SARAH CHAMBERLAIN
memoryArtifacts.description:
JOSEPH DAVIS AND SARAH CHAMBERLAIN 
Joseph Davis, born 12 Oct 1647, Roxbury, Suffolk, Mass. Married 28 Oct 1670, Sarah Chamberlain, at Roxbury, Mass. Died 25 Dec 1717, Roxbury, Suffolk, Mass. ORD: Bap …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joseph Davis於1647年10月12日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利時，他的父親William Davis27歲，母親Elizabeth Chamberlain26歲。他於1670年10月28日與Sarah Chamberlain在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他去世於1717年12月25日在他的家鄉享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joseph Davis于1647年10月12日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利时，他的父亲William Davis27岁，母亲Elizabeth Chamberlain26岁。他于1670年10月28日与Sarah Chamberlain在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和9个女儿的父母。他去世于1717年12月25日在他的家乡享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Davis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: patronymic meaning ‘Dafydd's (son)’, equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd, the Welsh form of David . The spelling Davis is more typical in southwestern England northwards as far as Lancashire, where the frequency of the surname largely reflects Welsh migration, but may sometimes represent a native English surname based on Davy (compare Davies ). Davis (including in the sense 2 below) is the eighth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans.
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Daibhéid ‘son of David’; see McDevitt . Compare Davies .

History: John Davis or Davys (c. 1550–1605) was an English navigator who searched for the Northwest Passage. — By the 18th century there were numerous persons named Davis in America, including the jurist John Davis, born in 1761 in Plymouth, MA, and Henry Davis, a clergyman and college president, who was born in 1771 in East Hampton, NY. — Jefferson Davis, born in 1808 in KY, was president of the Confederate States of America from 1861 to 1865.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Davies
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of Davis , a patronymic equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd ‘Dafydd's son’. The name is especially numerous in south Wales. This form of the name probably shows the influence of Davy , the common English form of the personal name David in the late medieval and early modern periods (see Davey ).

variants.name: Daves
meanings: Welsh and English: probably a variant of   Davis   or   Davies  .
In some cases also German (Däves): from a shortened form of the personal name Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is very rare in Germany.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of German   Tewes  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Joseph Davis was born on 12 October 1647, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, William Davis, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth Chamberlain, was 26. He married Sarah Chamberlain on 28 October 1670, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 9 daughters. He died on 25 December 1717, in his hometown, at the age of 70.

Il a épousé Sarah Chamberlain le 28 octobre 1670, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 9 filles. Il est décédé le 25 décembre 1717, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joseph Davis am 12. Oktober 1647 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Davis 27 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Chamberlain 26. Er hat am 28. Oktober 1670 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Sarah Chamberlain geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 9 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Dezember 1717 im Alter von 70 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Davis è nato il il 12 ottobre 1647 nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, William Davis, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Chamberlain, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Chamberlain il 28 ottobre 1670, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 9 figlie. Morì il 25 dicembre 1717, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joseph Davisが1647年10月12日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーで生まれた時，父William Davisは27歳，母Elizabeth Chamberlainは26歳であった。1670年10月28日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーでSarah Chamberlainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1717年12月25日に故郷で70歳で死去した。

ko.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이
ko.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay, Bri: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이
ko.Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴
ko.Muddy River, Boston, Suffolk, Massachuse: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴 무디 라이버
ko.Pomfret, Windham, Colony of Connecticut,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 윈드함 폼프렛
ko.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts,: 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이
ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Davis(이)가 1647년 10월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Davis(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Chamberlain(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1670년 10월 28일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 Sarah Chamberlain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1717년 12월 25일 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Davis nasceu em 12 de outubro de 1647, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, William Davis, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Chamberlain, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Chamberlain em 28 de outubro de 1670, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 9 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de dezembro de 1717, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joseph Davis родился 12 октября 1647 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени William Davis было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Chamberlain, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Chamberlain 28 октября 1670 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 9 дочерей. Он умер 25 декабря 1717 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет.

Murió el 25 de diciembre de 1717, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años.
